ItacPersonItem.Notes: Poet. The brother of Francesco Maria Sforza, Bishop of Conversano, Muzio moved to Venice in 1584. His portrait is taken from Delle rime del signor Muzio Sforza parte terza (Venice: Salicato, 1590). Luigi Russo, Muzio Sforza poeta monopolitano tra Rinascenza e Controriforma (Bari: Puglia Grafica Sud, 1985), affirms that he joined the Accademia degli Uranici. His names is also associated to the Accademia Olimpica, see Maddalena Campiglia, Flori, A Pastoral Drama. A bilingual edition, ed. Virginia Cox and Lisa Sampson, transl. Virginia Cox (Chicago: The University of Chicago Press, 2004), p. 321 n.84.
ItacPersonItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
